text:
There once was a wise old birderman who knew everything about eggs. Every Easter came time when he sat on his nest just perfect. Finally Sunday came just his usual Sunday afternoons'.

'Oh!' he cried. 'That's my biggest goose!' That night goose wouldn't coop. He flew around.

'Come here!' shouted Owl.

'I'll fly.' Said goose. Owl flew away spinning.

There came a fox.

'Oh dear!' cried silly goose. Fox grabbed goose.

'So you've taken me!' he howled. Fox jumped back.

'Aren't you frozen?' owl said.

'I am.' Said fox.

'Ooh!' goose cried. Fox laughed.

'I'll take you south.'

'You won't.'

'I will!' Fox snarled. He grabbed goose's leg anyway.

'I'd think!' said goose.

He ran fast. Finally dove underground.

'ooh!' goose screamed.

'I'll find you.' Said owl. Owls found him soon.

'You
